The Vatican has its own astronomy observatory, the Vatican Observatory, which is one of the oldest astronomical research institutions in the world.
The oldest continuously functioning hospital in the world, the Ospedale di Santo Spirito in Sassia, was founded by Catholics in Rome.
The Big Bang Theory was first proposed by a Catholic priest and physicist, Fr. Georges Lemaître.
There are over 10,000 named saints in the Catholic Church.
The Vatican City is the smallest independent state in the world by both area and population.
The Pope has a dedicated Twitter account, @Pontifex, which translates to 'bridge builder' in Latin.
The Swiss Guard, the military force responsible for the safety of the Pope, has been serving the Vatican since 1506.
A Catholic monk named Gregor Mendel is widely recognized as the founder of the modern science of genetics.
J.R.R. Tolkien, the author of The Lord of the Rings, was a devout Catholic and wove deep theological themes into his writing.
St. Peter's Basilica is the largest church in the world by interior measure.
The Catholic Church is the largest non-government provider of health care and education in the world.
The Gutenberg Bible, the first major book printed using mass-produced movable metal type in Europe, was a Catholic Latin Bible.
Notre-Dame Cathedral in Paris took over 180 years to build and is a masterpiece of French Gothic architecture.
The word 'Catholic' comes from the Greek word 'katholikos,' meaning 'universal'.
St. Lucia is the only country in the world named after a female Catholic saint.
Louis Pasteur, who developed the pasteurization process and the rabies vaccine, was a devout Catholic.
The cross is the most widely recognized religious symbol in the world.
Popes do not get paid a salary; their needs are provided by the Vatican.
The largest bell in the world is the Tsar Bell in Russia, but the largest ringing church bell is St. Peter's Bell in the Cologne Cathedral.
There are more than 1.3 billion baptized Catholics worldwide.
